Understanding Python Async

Python's async/await syntax has transformed how we write concurrent code. In this article, we'll explore the fundamentals of asyncio and how it can improve your application's performance.

The key insight is that async programming allows your code to handle multiple I/O operations without blocking. This is particularly useful for web applications, API clients, and data pipelines.

Getting Started

To begin using async in Python, you need to understand three core concepts: coroutines, event loops, and tasks. A coroutine is defined using the async def syntax and can be awaited to yield control back to the event loop.

The event loop is the central execution mechanism that manages and distributes the execution of different tasks. It keeps track of all running tasks and when a task is waiting for I/O, the loop switches to another task.
